title: Hermes-Relay pet manifest
description: Schema for pet.json — a side-loaded still or animated avatar ('pet') pack for Hermes-Relay. Encodes the loader's structural rules; on-disk checks (file existence, decodability) are enforced at load time. Visual QA rules such as PNG alpha, safe-box padding, and frame registration are documented in docs/pet-spec.md.

schemaVersion.description: Manifest schema version. This build supports 1.

id.description: Stable identity + persistence key. Falls back to the pack directory name if blank.

label.description: Display name in the avatar picker. Falls back to id.

description.description: Free-text description, shown in the picker.

states.description: Animation clips keyed by state/reaction name. An 'idle' clip is required; every other state falls back through it. A one-item frames list is valid and displays a still for that state.

idle.description: Required. Shown when waiting between turns; the fallback for every other state.

working.description: Tool-use overlay. Shipping this clip is what lights the Tools badge.

writing.description: Producing output text (streaming). Preferred alias for the streaming state.

streaming.description: Producing output text. 'writing' is the friendlier alias.

greet.description: One-shot reaction when the pet first appears.

done.description: One-shot reaction when a productive turn finishes (streaming/speaking -> idle).

defaults.description: Fallback clip for any state with no usable clip in 'states'.

reactive.description: Which live signals the pet honors. Drives the picker capability badge.

voice.description: Voice amplitude gives a subtle scale 'bounce' while speaking/listening. Advertises Voice.

tools.description: Ignored: tool reactivity is driven by shipping a 'working' clip, not this flag. The badge follows the clip, so a bare declaration can't over-promise.

intensity.description: The active clip plays faster as agent activity ramps (up to ~1.6x at peak). Advertises Activity.

clip.description: One animation clip: either a 'frames' list (one image per frame) OR a 'sheet' grid (one image, sliced into cells). Loops while its state is active; a one-frame clip displays as a still. For sheets, frameWidth/frameHeight are cell canvas dimensions; visible art should leave internal transparent padding.

frames.description: Frame image files, in play order. A one-item list is a valid static clip. Paths must stay inside the pack directory.

sheet.description: A single sprite-sheet image, sliced left-to-right, top-to-bottom into a grid of cells. Path must stay inside the pack directory.

frameWidth.description: Sprite-sheet cell canvas width, in pixels. This is not the visible character width; leave safe transparent padding inside each cell.

frameHeight.description: Sprite-sheet cell canvas height, in pixels. This is not the visible character height; leave safe transparent padding inside each cell.

frameCount.description: Number of cells in the sheet (rows x columns). Clamped to the sheet's real capacity at load.

fps.description: Frames per second; clamped to 1-60 at load.
